persons.bluf: The Financial Services chair who ran the high-profile bank-CEO and crypto/stablecoin oversight hearings (including questioning Zuckerberg on Libra/Diem and the FTX collapse). Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence she is the leading recent legislative skeptic of Big Tech's financial ambitions and crypto consumer risk.
persons.drivers: Consumer-protection and civil-rights conviction; anti-concentrated-power orientation; oversight zeal.
persons.worldview: Big Tech and crypto threaten consumers and the financial system without strong guardrails.
persons.decision_style: Hearing-forward accountability; combative questioning; legislative-framework attempts.
persons.track_record: Libra/Diem pushback on Meta; FTX-collapse and stablecoin hearings; bank-CEO accountability sessions; ranking member from 2023.
persons.vulnerabilities: Stablecoin-framework negotiations stalled; minority status after 2022.
persons.relationships: Sparred with Zuckerberg (Meta) and the crypto industry; counterpart to McHenry/Hill.
persons.outlook: On-thesis legislative skeptic of Big Tech finance and crypto.

persons.name: Patrick McHenry
persons.bluf: The crypto-fluent chair who drove the first comprehensive US digital-asset market-structure bill (FIT21) and the stablecoin framework through the House. Assess with HIGH confidence he is the principal legislative architect of the crypto-regulatory framework now advancing — the legislative counterpart to the 2025 regulator accommodation.
persons.drivers: Pro-innovation, market-structure conviction; capital-formation focus; legacy-through-crypto-framework.
persons.worldview: Clear digital-asset rules will keep the industry onshore; the SEC's enforcement approach failed.
persons.decision_style: Coalition-building, technically engaged; moved FIT21 with bipartisan votes.
persons.track_record: FIT21 (the 2024 crypto market-structure bill); stablecoin-framework negotiations; briefly acting Speaker (Oct 2023); retired 2025.
persons.vulnerabilities: The framework's consumer-protection critiques; industry-alignment optics.
persons.relationships: Counterpart to Waters; legislative ally of the crypto industry (Coinbase/Armstrong, Circle/Allaire).
persons.outlook: On-thesis architect of the crypto-market-structure legislation.

persons.name: French Hill
persons.bluf: The former banker and Treasury official chairing Financial Services as the crypto-market-structure and stablecoin bills move toward enactment. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence he is the current House fulcrum for the digital-asset legislative framework, succeeding McHenry's agenda.
persons.drivers: Banking-industry fluency (ex-bank CEO, ex-Treasury); pro-market, pro-crypto-framework orientation.
persons.worldview: Sound digital-asset rules and lighter bank regulation strengthen US finance.
persons.decision_style: Technically grounded; advancing the market-structure and stablecoin bills.
persons.track_record: Treasury (Bush 41) and community-banking background; 2025 chair shepherding crypto market-structure/stablecoin legislation.
persons.vulnerabilities: Banker-background and industry-alignment optics.
persons.relationships: House counterpart to Tim Scott's Senate Banking; continues McHenry's framework.
persons.outlook: On-thesis current driver of the crypto legislative framework.

persons.name: Elizabeth Warren
persons.role: US Senator; member, Senate Banking Committee
persons.bluf: The bankruptcy-law scholar who conceived the CFPB and became the Senate's foremost antagonist of Wall Street, private equity, and crypto. Assess with HIGH confidence she is the leading legislative voice of the concentrated-financial-power critique that the project's structural analysis parallels — the counter-pole to the deregulation lineage.
persons.drivers: Consumer-protection conviction; anti-concentrated-power conviction; rigorous structural analysis.
persons.worldview: Markets are rigged toward the powerful; aggressive regulation and antitrust are correctives.
persons.decision_style: Evidence-driven, relentless oversight; coined the post-crisis accountability framing.
persons.track_record: Conceived and helped stand up the CFPB; the 2023 SVB-failure and bank-deregulation critiques; the 'anti-crypto army' stance; private-equity and Fed-oversight pressure.
persons.vulnerabilities: Minority leverage in the deregulatory era; the crypto industry's organized opposition.
persons.relationships: Aligned with Brown/Sanders; antagonist of the crypto industry and big banks.
persons.outlook: On-thesis keystone of the concentrated-power counter-critique.

persons.name: Bernie Sanders
persons.role: US Senator; Chair/Ranking, Senate Budget and HELP Committees
persons.bluf: The democratic-socialist senator whose 'billionaire class' and 'too big to fail' critiques shaped the modern inequality and financial-concentration debate. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence his framing is the populist-left articulation of the wealth-concentration dynamics the project's distributional themes track.
persons.drivers: Egalitarian conviction; anti-oligarchy framing; movement-building.
persons.worldview: Concentrated wealth and corporate power corrupt democracy and markets; structural redistribution is the remedy.
persons.decision_style: Movement-oriented, message-disciplined; legislative pressure via amendments and the bully pulpit.
persons.track_record: 'Break up the banks' and Fed-audit campaigns; wealth-tax and antitrust advocacy; two presidential runs shifting the discourse.
persons.vulnerabilities: Limited enacted legislation; the gap between rhetoric and legislative outcomes.
persons.relationships: Aligned with Warren on finance; the left pole of the concentration debate.
persons.outlook: On-thesis populist-left voice on wealth concentration.

persons.name: Ron Wyden
persons.role: US Senator; Chair/Ranking, Senate Finance; member, Senate Intelligence
persons.bluf: The Senate's foremost surveillance-and-privacy hawk, who pressed Clapper on bulk collection and has fought Section 702, data-broker sales, and warrantless tracking for over a decade. Assess with HIGH confidence he is the leading legislative check on the surveillance architecture the project tracks.
persons.drivers: Civil-liberties conviction; oversight tenacity; technology-and-privacy fluency.
persons.worldview: Mass surveillance and commercial data-harvesting threaten constitutional rights; transparency and warrants are essential.
persons.decision_style: Persistent oversight (his questioning preceded the Clapper 'least untruthful' answer); coalition across the aisle on privacy.
persons.track_record: Section 215/702 reform fights; exposing data-broker sales to government; the 'secret law' critique of FISA; Finance Committee tax/trade work.
persons.vulnerabilities: Surveillance authorities keep getting reauthorized; the data-broker loophole persists.
persons.relationships: Antagonist of Clapper/Hayden-era surveillance; ally of privacy advocates.
persons.outlook: On-thesis legislative check on surveillance and data exploitation.

persons.name: Mark Warner
persons.role: US Senator; Chair/Vice-Chair, Senate Intelligence Committee
persons.bluf: The tech-entrepreneur senator (co-founder of Nextel) who leads Senate Intelligence on China, semiconductors, TikTok, and AI/platform security. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence he is the key legislative node linking the intelligence committee to the technology-supply-chain and platform-governance fronts.
persons.drivers: Tech-industry fluency; national-security pragmatism; bipartisan dealmaking.
persons.worldview: Technology is the central arena of US-China competition; platforms and supply chains are security assets.
persons.decision_style: Pragmatic, bipartisan; bridges Silicon Valley and the intelligence community.
persons.track_record: Led Senate Intel's Russia investigation; CHIPS-Act advocacy; TikTok/RESTRICT Act push; AI-governance and platform-security work.
persons.vulnerabilities: Balancing tech-industry ties with regulatory oversight.
persons.relationships: Bridges the intelligence committee and the tech/chip industry; counterpart to the SSCI leadership lineage.
persons.outlook: On-thesis node connecting intelligence oversight and tech supply chains.

persons.name: Dianne Feinstein
persons.bluf: The long-serving Intelligence chair who produced the landmark Senate 'torture report' on CIA interrogation, yet defended bulk surveillance. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence her tenure embodied the tension between aggressive oversight of past abuses and accommodation of ongoing surveillance.
persons.drivers: Institutional-oversight duty; national-security centrism; legacy-through-investigation.
persons.worldview: Hold past abuses accountable, but bulk collection is a legitimate security tool.
persons.decision_style: Establishment-centrist; pursued the torture report while defending NSA programs (until the CIA-search dispute).
persons.track_record: The 2014 Senate Intelligence torture report; clashed with Brennan's CIA over the investigation; defended Section 215 until the CIA spied on her staff.
persons.vulnerabilities: The contradiction between the torture-report rigor and surveillance defense; late-tenure capacity questions.
persons.relationships: Antagonist of Brennan over the torture probe; node of the oversight-vs-surveillance tension.
persons.outlook: On-thesis figure of the oversight/surveillance contradiction.

persons.name: Richard Burr
persons.bluf: The Intelligence chair who led the bipartisan Russia-interference investigation, then stepped aside amid an insider-trading inquiry into well-timed pandemic stock sales. Assess with MODERATE confidence his case is a prominent congressional-trading-ethics exhibit.
persons.drivers: National-security institutionalism; bipartisan-investigation commitment; market participation.
persons.worldview: Sober, evidence-based intelligence oversight.
persons.decision_style: Bipartisan, low-key; the Russia-probe report was notably cross-party.
persons.track_record: Senate Intel Russia investigation; stepped aside from the chair (2020) during an SEC/DOJ probe of pandemic-timed stock sales (later closed without charges).
persons.vulnerabilities: The insider-trading-investigation optics; the congressional-trading-reform debate.
persons.relationships: Node in the congressional-stock-trading-ethics theme (with Pelosi-household trades).
persons.outlook: On-thesis congressional-trading-ethics exhibit.

persons.name: Adam Schiff
persons.role: Chair, House Intelligence Committee (2019–2023); US Senator (2025–)
persons.bluf: The House Intelligence chair who led the first Trump impeachment and the Russia/Ukraine investigations, now a senator. Assess with MODERATE confidence his tenure is a focal point of the politicization-of-intelligence-oversight conflict that defines the era.
persons.drivers: Prosecutorial conviction; institutional-accountability focus; partisan-combat resolve.
persons.worldview: Robust oversight is a check on executive abuse; the intelligence committees are guardrails.
persons.decision_style: Methodical, media-forward; led high-profile investigations and the first impeachment.
persons.track_record: First-impeachment lead manager; Russia/Ukraine investigations; elected to the Senate (2024).
persons.vulnerabilities: Intense partisan polarization around his role; contested oversight conclusions.
persons.relationships: Counterpart/antagonist of Nunes; node in the intelligence-oversight politicization.
persons.outlook: On-thesis figure in the oversight-politicization conflict.

persons.name: Devin Nunes
persons.role: Chair, House Intelligence Committee (2015–2019); CEO, Trump Media & Technology Group (2022–)
persons.bluf: The House Intelligence chair turned CEO of Trump Media (Truth Social, parent of the 'Truth.Fi' and crypto-treasury ventures) — a direct congressman-to-platform-capital node. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence his arc uniquely links intelligence oversight, the media-platform layer, and the administration's crypto-treasury ambitions.
persons.drivers: Partisan loyalty; combative oversight; post-Congress platform-and-capital ambition.
persons.worldview: The intelligence and media establishments are adversaries to be countered with aligned platforms.
persons.decision_style: Confrontational; left Congress to run a politically aligned media/finance venture.
persons.track_record: House Intel chair (the 'Nunes memo' on FISA surveillance); since 2022 CEO of Trump Media & Technology Group, which has pursued Bitcoin-treasury and 'Truth.Fi' crypto/fintech ventures.
persons.vulnerabilities: TMTG's volatile valuation and SPAC/disclosure scrutiny; the oversight-to-platform-capital conflict optics.
persons.relationships: Connects the intelligence-oversight, media-platform, and crypto-treasury threads (links to the Trump/crypto nodes).
persons.outlook: On-thesis node bridging oversight, media platforms, and crypto capital.

persons.name: Cynthia Lummis
persons.role: US Senator; Chair, Senate Banking Digital Assets Subcommittee
persons.bluf: The Senate's leading Bitcoin advocate and author of the strategic-Bitcoin-reserve and crypto-market-structure proposals. Assess with HIGH confidence she is the foremost legislative champion of the digital-asset agenda, personally invested in the thesis she legislates.
persons.drivers: Hard-money/Bitcoin conviction; Wyoming digital-asset-hub boosterism; sound-money ideology.
persons.worldview: Bitcoin is sound money and a strategic reserve asset; clear crypto rules will onshore the industry.
persons.decision_style: Advocacy-driven, framework-building; the BITCOIN Act (strategic reserve) and Lummis-Gillibrand market-structure bill.
persons.track_record: Lummis-Gillibrand crypto framework; strategic-Bitcoin-reserve proposal; Wyoming's crypto-banking (SPDI) charters; personally holds Bitcoin.
persons.vulnerabilities: Personal-holdings conflict optics; the reserve proposal's fiscal and volatility critiques.
persons.relationships: Aligned with the crypto industry (Saylor's Bitcoin-reserve thesis); counterpart to Warren's skepticism.
persons.outlook: On-thesis legislative champion of the Bitcoin/crypto agenda.

persons.name: Tom Emmer
persons.bluf: The House Majority Whip and a leading crypto advocate who authored anti-CBDC legislation and championed digital-asset deregulation. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence he is the House-leadership-level driver of the pro-crypto, anti-central-bank-digital-currency agenda.
persons.drivers: Crypto-innovation conviction; anti-surveillance-CBDC framing; deregulatory ideology.
persons.worldview: Permissionless crypto good; a government CBDC is a surveillance threat to be barred.
persons.decision_style: Whip-level coalition-building; framework and anti-CBDC bill advocacy.
persons.track_record: CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act; crypto-market-structure support; co-chairs the Congressional Blockchain Caucus.
persons.vulnerabilities: Industry-alignment optics; the deregulation-vs-consumer-protection tension.
persons.relationships: Aligns the House leadership with the crypto industry and the anti-CBDC position (intersecting Giancarlo's pro-CBDC stance).
persons.outlook: On-thesis House-leadership driver of crypto and anti-CBDC policy.

persons.name: Jim Jordan
persons.bluf: The Judiciary chair leading the 'weaponization' and Big-Tech-'censorship' investigations into platform-government content moderation. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence his subpoenas of platforms and agencies are central to the ad-censorship and platform-governance threads the project tracks.
persons.drivers: Anti-establishment conviction; combative oversight; free-speech-grievance framing.
persons.worldview: The federal government and Big Tech colluded to suppress disfavored speech; aggressive oversight is the remedy.
persons.decision_style: Confrontational subpoena-driven investigation; the 'Weaponization' subcommittee.
persons.track_record: Judiciary chair investigating platform content moderation, the 'censorship-industrial complex' framing, and antitrust/Section-230 debates.
persons.vulnerabilities: Partisan-investigation critiques; contested factual claims.
persons.relationships: Antagonist of the platform-moderation apparatus; intersects the Meta/ad-censorship blocks.
persons.outlook: On-thesis driver of the platform-governance/censorship investigations.

persons.name: Josh Hawley
persons.role: US Senator; member, Senate Judiciary Committee
persons.bluf: The Senate's most aggressive Big-Tech antagonist on antitrust, Section 230, child safety, and AI accountability. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence he is a leading legislative voice for breaking up and regulating the tech platforms central to the AI-concentration thesis.
persons.drivers: Populist-conservative conviction; anti-monopoly framing; ambition.
persons.worldview: Big Tech is an unaccountable monopoly power harming children, competition, and democracy.
persons.decision_style: Combative, legislation-and-hearing-forward; bipartisan on some tech bills.
persons.track_record: Antitrust and Section-230 reform bills; child-online-safety and AI-liability proposals; tech-CEO interrogations.
persons.vulnerabilities: Partisan polarization; mixed legislative success.
persons.relationships: Sometimes aligns with Warren/Blumenthal on tech; antagonist of the platform CEOs in the persons list.
persons.outlook: On-thesis legislative antagonist of Big Tech concentration.

persons.name: John McCain
persons.bluf: The Armed Services chair and defense-establishment conscience whose hawkish internationalism shaped two decades of military and intelligence policy. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence his defense-authorization leadership and Russia/China hawkishness frame the defense-industrial and allied-intelligence themes.
persons.drivers: Service-and-honor ethos; internationalist conviction; defense-establishment institutionalism.
persons.worldview: American military primacy and alliances deter authoritarian powers; defense investment is paramount.
persons.decision_style: Independent maverick within hawkish bounds; NDAA-driven oversight.
persons.track_record: Armed Services chair (NDAA leadership); defense-procurement reform; Russia/China hawkishness; the deciding 2017 ACA-repeal vote.
persons.vulnerabilities: Some interventions criticized in hindsight; intra-party friction.
persons.relationships: Defense-establishment anchor; counterpart to the armed-services lineage.
persons.outlook: On-thesis figure for the defense-industrial and alliance themes.

persons.name: Jack Reed
persons.bluf: The West Point-trained senior Armed Services leader steering defense authorization through the AI-defense, Ukraine, and Indo-Pacific era. Assess with MODERATE confidence he is the current legislative fulcrum for the defense-industrial-base and military-AI investments the project tracks.
persons.drivers: Military-professional duty; institutional defense-policy focus; bipartisan pragmatism.
persons.worldview: Sustained defense investment and alliances are essential; technology (AI, autonomy) is the new frontier.
persons.decision_style: Detail-oriented NDAA leadership; low-profile institutionalism.
persons.track_record: Armed Services chair/ranking through Ukraine aid, AUKUS, Indo-Pacific deterrence, and defense-AI authorization.
persons.vulnerabilities: Defense-budget and procurement-efficiency pressures.
persons.relationships: Counterpart to Wicker/Rogers; node of defense-authorization policy.
persons.outlook: On-thesis current driver of defense-industrial and military-AI policy.

persons.name: Ro Khanna
persons.role: US Representative (Silicon Valley); member, House Armed Services
persons.bluf: The Silicon Valley congressman bridging the tech industry and progressive economic policy, with deep ties to the operators in the persons list. Assess with MODERATE confidence he is a key legislative interlocutor between Big Tech/AI capital and Washington policy.
persons.drivers: Tech-industry constituency service; progressive-economic conviction; bridge-building ambition.
persons.worldview: Technology can be harnessed for broad prosperity with the right policy; pragmatic engagement over rupture.
persons.decision_style: Coalition-bridging; comfortable with both tech executives and labor.
persons.track_record: Represents Apple/Google/Nvidia's district; AI, chips, and tech-labor policy engagement; foreign-policy restraint advocacy.
persons.vulnerabilities: The tension between tech-donor ties and progressive positions.
persons.relationships: Direct interlocutor with the Silicon Valley operators (Huang, Pichai, etc.).
persons.outlook: On-thesis legislative bridge to AI/tech capital.

persons.name: Do Kwon
persons.bluf: The architect of the largest algorithmic-stablecoin collapse in history. Assess with HIGH confidence the TerraUSD/Luna design was a known-fragile reflexive peg whose ~$40-60B implosion (May 2022) triggered the broader crypto contagion; charged with fraud by the SEC and DOJ.
persons.drivers: Founder grandiosity and conviction; combative defiance of skeptics ('I don't debate the poor'); reflexive-tokenomics belief.
persons.worldview: Algorithmic, collateral-light money can hold a peg through market incentives - a thesis repeatedly falsified before and by Terra.
persons.decision_style: Aggressive, dismissive of risk warnings; doubled down (the LFG Bitcoin reserve) as the peg broke.
persons.track_record: Co-created earlier failed algos (Basis Cash, pseudonymously) then Terra/Anchor's ~20% yield; the May-2022 death spiral; arrested in Montenegro (2023); Terraform settled with the SEC for ~$4.5B (2024); extradition/criminal proceedings.
persons.vulnerabilities: Fraud charges; the falsified design thesis; flight and detention.
persons.relationships: Catalyst of the 2022 contagion (Three Arrows, Celsius, Voyager); the canonical algorithmic-stablecoin cautionary node.
persons.outlook: On-thesis archetype of the algorithmic-stablecoin failure mode.

persons.name: Justin Sun
persons.role: Founder, Tron; USDD; major crypto investor
persons.bluf: The Tron founder behind the persistently under-pegged USDD stablecoin and a serial promoter at the center of multiple controversies - including a large stake in the Trump family's World Liberty Financial. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence he is a recurring node linking algorithmic-stablecoin fragility, exchange controversy, and the WLFI/USD1 conflict.
persons.drivers: Relentless self-promotion; regulatory-arbitrage agility; access-buying via large, visible investments.
persons.worldview: Attention and capital deployment are strategy; rules are jurisdictional puzzles to route around.
persons.decision_style: Publicity-maximizing, opportunistic; large symbolic buys (the WLFI stake, the $6.2M banana art).
persons.track_record: Founded Tron and USDD (sub-peg through 2022 stress); SEC fraud/market-manipulation charges (2023, later paused amid settlement talks); HTX/Huobi ties; became the largest disclosed WLFI investor (~$75M+), proximate to the USD1 conflict.
persons.vulnerabilities: SEC charges; reserve-transparency questions on USDD; the WLFI-stake conflict optics.
persons.relationships: Connects USDD (algo fragility), HTX, and the WLFI/USD1 Trump-family node; counterpart to Tether/Circle issuers.
persons.outlook: On-thesis recurring node across algo stablecoins and the WLFI conflict.

persons.name: Mario Draghi
persons.role: President, European Central Bank (2011–2019); PM of Italy (2021–2022)
persons.bluf: The central banker whose 'whatever it takes' (Jul 2012) ended the euro crisis with three words and no immediate spending. Assess with HIGH confidence he is the archetype of credible verbal intervention - and of the OMT/QE regime that anchored European asset prices for a decade.
persons.drivers: Institutional-survival conviction (saving the euro); technocratic mastery; credibility-as-tool.
persons.worldview: Central-bank credibility can substitute for fiscal action; the currency union must be defended at any cost.
persons.decision_style: Calculated, minimalist signaling backed by unlimited capacity (OMT was never used - the threat sufficed).
persons.track_record: 'Whatever it takes' + OMT; negative rates and QE; later Italy PM and author of the 2024 EU-competitiveness report warning Europe is falling behind on tech/AI.
persons.vulnerabilities: The QE-forever critique; Europe's structural stagnation his report diagnosed.
persons.relationships: ECB lineage to Lagarde; counterpart to the Fed's post-2012 regime.
persons.outlook: On-thesis model of credibility-driven monetary backstops.

persons.name: Haruhiko Kuroda
persons.bluf: The architect of the most extreme monetary experiment in the developed world - QQE, negative rates, and yield-curve control. Assess with HIGH confidence the BoJ's decade of pinned JGB yields is the engine of the global yen carry trade, the single largest source of the cheap leverage the project tracks as a system-wide fragility.
persons.drivers: Deflation-defeat mission; reflationist conviction; institutional persistence.
persons.worldview: Aggressive, sustained easing can break a deflationary mindset where gradualism failed.
persons.decision_style: Bold, all-in (QQE 'bazooka', then YCC); tolerated a vast BoJ balance sheet (>100% of GDP) and distorted bond market.
persons.track_record: QQE (2013), negative rates (2016), yield-curve control (2016) capping the 10Y; the BoJ became the dominant JGB and a major ETF holder.
persons.vulnerabilities: The exit problem he bequeathed: unwinding YCC risks a global carry-unwind (the Aug-2024 shock previewed it).
persons.relationships: His regime is the upstream source of the yen-carry leverage feeding global risk assets; handed the exit to Ueda.
persons.outlook: On-thesis origin of the carry-trade leverage and the latent unwind risk.

persons.name: Kazuo Ueda
persons.bluf: The academic governor tasked with the most dangerous exit in finance - dismantling YCC and negative rates without detonating the yen carry trade. Assess with HIGH confidence his pace of normalization is a live global-risk variable, since a disorderly exit forces a worldwide carry unwind.
persons.drivers: Scholarly caution; financial-stability priority; aversion to a destabilizing shock.
persons.worldview: Normalize gradually; the exit must avoid triggering the very instability the long easing created.
persons.decision_style: Deliberate, communication-sensitive; ended negative rates and YCC cautiously; the Aug-2024 hike + market crash taught him the system's sensitivity.
persons.track_record: Ended negative rates and YCC (2024); incremental hikes; managing the yen and JGB normalization against carry-unwind risk.
persons.vulnerabilities: Caught between domestic inflation/yen weakness and the global carry-unwind tail risk.
persons.relationships: Inherited Kuroda's experiment; his moves ripple into global risk assets and the AI-equity leverage chain.
persons.outlook: On-thesis pivot for the carry-unwind timing risk.

persons.name: Mark Carney
persons.role: Governor, Bank of England (2013–2020) & Bank of Canada (2008–2013); PM of Canada (2025–)
persons.bluf: The only person to run two G7 central banks, the architect of climate-finance (GFANZ), and now Canada's PM. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence he embodies the fusion of central banking, climate-capital mobilization, and elected power - a singular monetary-to-political arc.
persons.drivers: Technocratic ambition; climate-finance conviction; crisis-management pedigree.
persons.worldview: Markets and central banks should be mobilized for systemic goals (stability, then climate transition).
persons.decision_style: Polished, forward-guidance-pioneering; built the net-zero-finance coalition (GFANZ).
persons.track_record: Steered Canada through 2008 and the UK through Brexit; pioneered forward guidance; chaired the FSB; launched GFANZ; UN climate-finance envoy; elected Canadian PM (2025).
persons.vulnerabilities: GFANZ fractured as banks exited; the central-banker-to-politician transition's credibility tests.
persons.relationships: FSB/central-bank network; climate-finance node tying capital allocation to policy.
persons.outlook: On-thesis figure for the central-bank-to-capital-mobilization-to-power pathway.
persons.legitimacy_bearing: High central-banker credibility (BoC + BoE) carried into Canadian politics - legitimacy as the 'safe-hands technocrat,' an asset that erodes if markets or voters read managerialism without mandate.

persons.name: Andrew Bailey
persons.bluf: The BoE governor who managed the 2022 gilt/LDI crisis - the clearest recent demonstration that pension-fund leverage can force emergency central-bank intervention. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence that episode is a template for hidden-leverage blowups in the non-bank financial system.
persons.drivers: Financial-stability mandate; institutional caution; crisis-containment focus.
persons.worldview: The central bank is the backstop of last resort when leverage in the shadows breaks.
persons.decision_style: Reactive-stabilizing; the Sep-2022 emergency gilt purchases (after the Truss mini-budget) halted an LDI doom-loop.
persons.track_record: COVID QE; 2022 inflation tightening; the LDI/gilt intervention; ongoing non-bank-leverage scrutiny.
persons.vulnerabilities: Criticized on inflation timing; the LDI crisis exposed pension-system fragility he then had to rescue.
persons.relationships: BoE lineage from Carney; the LDI episode is a non-bank-leverage cross-reference for the private-credit blocks.
persons.outlook: On-thesis case of hidden-leverage forcing central-bank rescue.

persons.name: Pan Gongsheng
persons.bluf: The PBOC governor steering Chinese monetary policy through the property-debt deflation and the yuan-internationalization push. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence he is the counterpart node to the Fed in the dollar-vs-yuan and de-dollarization dynamics the project tracks.
persons.drivers: Party-aligned financial-stability mandate; yuan-internationalization goal; deflation/property-deleveraging management.
persons.worldview: Managed liberalization; gradual yuan internationalization (CIPS, mBridge) without losing capital control.
persons.decision_style: Controlled, gradualist; targeted easing amid the property bust; FX-stability defense.
persons.track_record: Managing the property-debt deflation, local-government debt, and yuan stability; advancing cross-border yuan settlement (CIPS) and the digital yuan (e-CNY).
persons.vulnerabilities: The property-debt overhang; the tension between stimulus and yuan stability; capital-flight pressure.
persons.relationships: Counterpart to Powell in the monetary-bloc contest; node in the CBDC/de-dollarization arc.
persons.outlook: On-thesis monetary node for the dollar-yuan and CBDC dynamics.

persons.name: Elvira Nabiullina
persons.bluf: The technocrat who kept the Russian financial system functioning through the 2022 sanctions shock - capital controls, an emergency rate spike to 20%, and ruble stabilization. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence she is the live case study in central-banking under maximal financial warfare.
persons.drivers: Technocratic professionalism; institutional duty under a regime she reportedly tried to leave; stability mandate.
persons.worldview: Orthodox central banking applied under siege; defend the currency and the banking system at all costs.
persons.decision_style: Decisive crisis-management (capital controls, rate spike, FX measures); reportedly sought to resign post-invasion but stayed.
persons.track_record: Cleaned up the Russian banking sector pre-war; managed the 2014 and 2022 sanctions shocks; built the SPFS payment-messaging alternative to SWIFT.
persons.vulnerabilities: Serving a sanctioned war economy; frozen reserves (~$300B) abroad; the moral-vs-technocratic tension.
persons.relationships: Counterpart in the financial-warfare/de-dollarization arc; SPFS/mBridge node.
persons.outlook: On-thesis case of central banking under sanctions and the SWIFT-alternative push.

persons.name: Raghuram Rajan
persons.role: Governor, Reserve Bank of India (2013–2016); IMF Chief Economist (2003–2007)
persons.bluf: The economist who warned of systemic financial risk at Jackson Hole in 2005 - and was dismissed before being vindicated by 2008. Assess with HIGH confidence he is the credible Cassandra of financial fragility, a recurring intellectual reference for the kind of hidden-risk buildup the project maps.
persons.drivers: Analytical independence; willingness to be unpopular; systemic-risk focus.
persons.worldview: Financial innovation and incentives can build catastrophic hidden risk that markets and policymakers ignore until too late.
persons.decision_style: Rigorous, contrarian, candid; cleaned up Indian bank NPAs as RBI governor.
persons.track_record: The 2005 'Has Financial Development Made the World Riskier?' warning (mocked, then vindicated); RBI inflation-targeting and bank-cleanup; prolific public intellectual.
persons.vulnerabilities: Pushed out of the RBI amid political friction; the perennial fate of the early warner.
persons.relationships: Intellectual kin to Born/Bair/Burry as a vindicated risk-Cassandra.
persons.outlook: On-thesis reference for credible early warning of systemic fragility.

persons.name: Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan (MBZ)
persons.role: President of the UAE; Ruler of Abu Dhabi
persons.bluf: The UAE president orchestrating one of the world's most aggressive sovereign AI-and-capital strategies - G42, MGX, Mubadala, ADIA - with his brother Tahnoon running the operational layer. Assess with HIGH confidence he is the apex node of the Gulf-sovereign-AI-capital loop the project tracks (USD1, Stargate-UAE, the Microsoft-G42 deal).
persons.drivers: Post-oil diversification imperative; strategic autonomy between the US and China; dynastic state-building.
persons.worldview: Convert hydrocarbon wealth into AI, compute, and capital influence; hedge between great powers.
persons.decision_style: Long-horizon, top-down, capital-deploying; centralizes strategy in the ruling family.
persons.track_record: Built ADIA/Mubadala into trillion-dollar funds; backed G42 (which took a $1.5B Microsoft investment and divested Huawei gear under US pressure); MGX (the $2B Binance/USD1 deal, Stargate-UAE); the AI campus ambitions.
persons.vulnerabilities: US-China balancing under CHIPS export controls; concentration and governance opacity; the USD1 conflict exposure.
persons.relationships: Brother of Tahnoon (profiled); ties to Trump-admin (MGX/USD1, Witkoff), Microsoft, OpenAI/Stargate, Nvidia.
persons.outlook: On-thesis apex of the Gulf-AI-capital strategy.

persons.name: Emmanuel Macron
persons.bluf: The French president pushing 'European sovereignty' in tech, AI, and defense - backing Mistral AI and nuclear-powered compute as a third pole between the US and China. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence he is the leading European-strategic-autonomy voice in the AI-capital and chip-dependency debates.
persons.drivers: European-autonomy conviction; reformist ambition; great-power-relevance aspiration for France/EU.
persons.worldview: Europe must build sovereign AI, energy, and defense capacity or be a vassal of the US and China.
persons.decision_style: Centralizing, initiative-heavy ('Jupiterian'); hosts AI summits; backs national champions (Mistral).
persons.track_record: EU strategic-autonomy push; Mistral AI backing; nuclear-energy revival for compute; the 2025 Paris AI Summit; domestic-reform turbulence.
persons.vulnerabilities: Domestic political weakness; the gap between sovereignty rhetoric and EU capability.
persons.relationships: EU node with von der Leyen/Lagarde; counterpart to US/China tech blocs.
persons.outlook: On-thesis voice for European AI/chip strategic autonomy.

persons.name: Angela Merkel
persons.bluf: The chancellor who anchored Europe through the euro and migration crises and bound German industry to Russian energy (Nord Stream) and Chinese demand. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence her energy-and-trade dependencies are the structural backdrop of Europe's current strategic vulnerability.
persons.drivers: Scientific pragmatism; stability-and-consensus preference; export-economy stewardship.
persons.worldview: Trade and interdependence promote stability (Wandel durch Handel) - a thesis the Ukraine war falsified.
persons.decision_style: Cautious, incremental, consensus-driven ('Merkeling'); crisis-reactive.
persons.track_record: Euro-crisis austerity leadership; 2015 migration opening; Nord Stream 1 & 2 (Russian gas dependence); deep China trade ties; nuclear phase-out.
persons.vulnerabilities: The energy-dependence and China-exposure legacies now seen as strategic errors.
persons.relationships: Bound German industry to Russian energy and Chinese demand - the dependencies the geopolitics blocks track.
persons.outlook: Historical architect of Europe's energy/trade dependency backdrop.

persons.name: Friedrich Merz
persons.bluf: The former BlackRock Germany chairman turned chancellor, steering Germany's rearmament and a historic fiscal-brake loosening for defense and infrastructure. Assess with MODERATE confidence his finance-industry pedigree and the debt-brake reform mark a structural shift in European fiscal and defense posture.
persons.drivers: Atlanticist, pro-market conviction; finance-industry worldview; strategic-rearmament resolve.
persons.worldview: Germany must rearm, invest, and reassert leadership; market-friendly but willing to break fiscal orthodoxy for defense.
persons.decision_style: Assertive, business-fluent; pushed the constitutional debt-brake exemption for defense/infrastructure.
persons.track_record: BlackRock Germany chairman; long CDU rivalry with Merkel; chancellor (2025) loosening the debt brake for a major defense/infrastructure spend.
persons.vulnerabilities: The BlackRock/finance-industry optics; coalition management; the fiscal-shift risks.
persons.relationships: BlackRock tie connects to Fink (profiled); EU node.
persons.outlook: On-thesis for the European fiscal/defense-spending shift.

persons.name: Giorgia Meloni
persons.bluf: The Italian PM who pulled Italy out of China's Belt and Road and positioned as a bridge between European, US, and Gulf interests. Assess with MODERATE confidence she is a pivotal swing figure in Europe's China-decoupling and Mediterranean-energy/AI alignment.
persons.drivers: Nationalist-conservative conviction; pragmatic Atlanticism; coalition-durability focus.
persons.worldview: Sovereigntist but pro-NATO/US; wary of China; Mediterranean-energy-hub ambition.
persons.decision_style: Pragmatic, alliance-balancing; exited BRI (2023) while courting US and Gulf capital.
persons.track_record: BRI exit; migration policy; energy diversification (the 'Mattei Plan' for Africa); pro-Ukraine.
persons.vulnerabilities: Italian debt and growth constraints; coalition politics.
persons.relationships: EU node; courts US/Gulf capital; China-decoupling swing.
persons.outlook: On-thesis swing figure in European China-decoupling.

persons.name: Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva
persons.bluf: The Brazilian president and leading voice for BRICS expansion and de-dollarization of trade. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence he is a principal driver of the multipolar-currency and Global-South-realignment dynamics the project tracks as a dollar-system stressor.
persons.drivers: Global-South solidarity; developmentalist conviction; multipolar-order ambition.
persons.worldview: The dollar-centric order disadvantages the Global South; BRICS and local-currency trade are correctives.
persons.decision_style: Coalition-building, rhetorically bold on de-dollarization; pragmatic on domestic economy.
persons.track_record: BRICS expansion advocacy; local-currency-trade and 'BRICS currency' discussion; Amazon/critical-minerals stewardship; commodity-export economy.
persons.vulnerabilities: The gap between de-dollarization rhetoric and feasibility; domestic fiscal pressures.
persons.relationships: BRICS node with Xi/Putin/Modi/Ramaphosa; de-dollarization arc.
persons.outlook: On-thesis driver of de-dollarization/multipolar-currency pressure.

persons.name: Prabowo Subianto
persons.bluf: The Indonesian president commanding the world's dominant nickel supply and a downstreaming strategy for EV/battery minerals. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence Indonesia's nickel-export policy under him is a critical-minerals chokepoint directly relevant to the battery/EV and critical-minerals blocks.
persons.drivers: Resource-nationalist developmentalism; strategic-autonomy ambition; military-strongman background.
persons.worldview: Indonesia should capture value by downstreaming its minerals, not exporting raw ore; hedge between powers.
persons.decision_style: Assertive, resource-leveraging; continued the nickel-ore export ban to force domestic processing (often via Chinese-partnered smelters).
persons.track_record: Nickel-ore export ban and downstreaming; courting US, China, and Gulf investment; defense-ministry background.
persons.vulnerabilities: Chinese dominance of the nickel-processing buildout; environmental and governance concerns.
persons.relationships: Critical-minerals chokepoint node; balances US/China/Gulf capital.
persons.outlook: On-thesis controller of the nickel supply chain.

persons.name: Anwar Ibrahim
persons.bluf: The Malaysian PM presiding over the Penang/Kulim semiconductor-packaging boom - the back-end assembly hub now central to the AI chip supply chain and the data-center buildout. Assess with MODERATE confidence Malaysia under him is a rising chokepoint in chip packaging and a magnet for AI-datacenter capital.
persons.drivers: Reformist-developmentalist conviction; non-aligned balancing; economic-modernization focus.
persons.worldview: Malaysia can capture AI-supply-chain value (packaging, data centers) by staying neutral between the US and China.
persons.decision_style: Coalition-balancing, investment-courting; attracts both US (Intel, GlobalFoundries) and Chinese capital.
persons.track_record: Penang/Kulim advanced-packaging investment wave; data-center FDI surge (Johor); semiconductor strategy; non-aligned diplomacy.
persons.vulnerabilities: US export-control pressure on Chinese transshipment; power/water constraints on data centers.
persons.relationships: Chip-packaging and data-center node; balances US/China.
persons.outlook: On-thesis rising node in chip back-end and AI infrastructure.

persons.name: Lawrence Wong
persons.bluf: The Singapore PM and former finance minister steering the region's premier financial and AI-infrastructure hub. Assess with MODERATE confidence Singapore under him is a key neutral node for capital, chip transshipment scrutiny, and data-center siting in the US-China contest.
persons.drivers: Technocratic continuity; hub-economy stewardship; strict non-alignment.
persons.worldview: Singapore thrives as a trusted neutral hub; survival depends on balancing the great powers.
persons.decision_style: Methodical, consensus-PAP technocracy; financial-stability and rule-of-law emphasis.
persons.track_record: Finance minister through COVID; PM (2024); managing Singapore as a wealth, fintech, and AI-datacenter hub amid chip-transshipment scrutiny.
persons.vulnerabilities: Exposure to US-China decoupling; chip-re-export-control pressure; data-center power limits.
persons.relationships: Financial-hub and AI-infrastructure node; neutral broker.
persons.outlook: On-thesis neutral hub for capital and AI infrastructure.

persons.name: Warren Buffett
persons.bluf: The value-investing patriarch whose record cash pile (>$300B+ by 2024-25) and equity-trimming are read as a market-top signal. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence Berkshire's defensive positioning is a closely-watched contrarian indicator against the AI-equity exuberance the project maps.
persons.drivers: Margin-of-safety discipline; long-horizon patience; aversion to overpaying.
persons.worldview: Buy wonderful businesses at fair prices; fear when others are greedy; cash is optionality.
persons.decision_style: Patient, contrarian, valuation-anchored; lets cash build when nothing is cheap.
persons.track_record: Decades of compounding; the Apple stake (then trimmed); record cash build and net equity selling into 2024-25; succession to Greg Abel.
persons.vulnerabilities: Cash-drag in a melt-up; the 'too cautious' critique; age/succession.
persons.relationships: Contrarian counterweight to the AI-momentum operators; the cash-pile-as-signal node.
persons.outlook: On-thesis defensive/contrarian indicator against the bubble.

persons.name: George Soros
persons.role: Founder, Soros Fund Management & Open Society Foundations
persons.bluf: The macro-trader whose theory of REFLEXIVITY - that perceptions and prices feed back on each other to create self-reinforcing booms and busts - is a direct intellectual frame for the project's analysis of the AI/credit reflexive loop. Assess with MODERATE confidence reflexivity is the most apt market-theory lens for the self-marking circular core.
persons.drivers: Reflexivity conviction; open-society ideology; macro-trading instinct.
persons.worldview: Markets are not efficient; participants' biased perceptions shape the fundamentals they then react to (reflexivity), producing bubbles and crashes.
persons.decision_style: Aggressive macro bets on reflexive turning points (the 1992 sterling break); fallibility-aware.
persons.track_record: 'Broke the Bank of England' (1992); the Quantum Fund record; 'The Alchemy of Finance' (reflexivity); Open Society philanthropy (a frequent target of conspiracy narratives - noted and excluded from claims here).
persons.vulnerabilities: Politicized target of disinformation; reflexivity is a frame, not a timing tool.
persons.relationships: Intellectual frame for the project's reflexive-loop thesis (self-marked value, M1-M4 reflexive marks).
persons.outlook: On-thesis conceptual lens (reflexivity) for the bubble dynamics.

persons.name: Chen Zhi
persons.bluf: The Cambodian tycoon at the center of the largest 'pig butchering' scam-compound empire and the largest crypto forfeiture in history. Assess with HIGH confidence (DOJ indictment + OFAC designation) he directed forced-labor fraud compounds; ~$15B in Bitcoin was seized and 146 Prince Group targets sanctioned.
persons.drivers: Wealth-and-power accumulation; state-adjacency and political protection; opacity via casinos, real estate, and crypto.
persons.worldview: Treat fraud as an industry — vertically integrate compounds, casinos, banking, and political cover.
persons.decision_style: Empire-building, protection-buying; layered corporate/offshore and on-chain laundering.
persons.track_record: Built Prince Group (real estate, Jin Bei casinos) as cover for forced-labor scam compounds; DOJ wire-fraud/money-laundering indictment (2025); OFAC 146-target designation + 4 named BTC addresses (~$1.8B); ~$15B BTC seized; reportedly arrested and moved to China.
persons.vulnerabilities: Indicted; sanctioned; seized assets; the on-chain trail (OFAC addresses) is permanent and public.
persons.relationships: Node linking the SE-Asia scam economy, casino/real-estate laundering, and (via Huione) the DPRK cyber-laundering map.
persons.outlook: On-thesis archetype of the industrialized-fraud + on-chain-laundering complex.

persons.name: Aravind Srinivas
persons.bluf: The AI-search challenger monetizing answers over links; raised at a multibillion valuation amid the search-disruption narrative.
persons.drivers: Founder ambition; speed; antagonizing Google's moat.
persons.worldview: AI-native answer engines replace the link economy.
persons.decision_style: Aggressive launches, provocative bids (offered to buy TikTok-US, Chrome).
persons.track_record: Perplexity growth + serial mega-rounds; publisher-content disputes.
persons.vulnerabilities: Burn rate; content/copyright suits; Google/OpenAI competition.
persons.relationships: Backed by Nvidia, Jeff Bezos, IVP; rival to Google/OpenAI.
persons.outlook: On-thesis AI-capex/valuation node.

persons.name: Arthur Mensch
persons.bluf: The face of European 'sovereign AI'; Mistral is France/EU's open-weight champion, backed by Macron-era industrial policy and Gulf/US capital.
persons.drivers: European strategic-autonomy conviction; open-weight philosophy; ex-DeepMind.
persons.worldview: Europe must own foundation models or be a vassal (Macron's thesis).
persons.decision_style: Open-weight releases; sovereignty framing; raised from a16z, Nvidia, MGX/ASML.
persons.track_record: Mistral models; EU-AI-Act positioning; ASML €1.3B+ strategic stake (2025).
persons.vulnerabilities: Compute/capital gap vs US hyperscalers; monetization.
persons.relationships: Macron-aligned; MGX/ASML backers tie to the Gulf-AI + chip threads.
persons.outlook: On-thesis European-sovereign-AI node.

persons.name: Emad Mostaque
persons.bluf: The open-source-AI evangelist whose Stability AI imploded financially — a cautionary case of narrative outrunning the balance sheet.
persons.drivers: Decentralization/open-model conviction; promotional intensity.
persons.worldview: Open models democratize AI; (critics: overpromised).
persons.decision_style: Rapid expansion, then a cash crisis and 2024 resignation.
persons.track_record: Stable Diffusion; Stability's near-insolvency + departures.
persons.vulnerabilities: Credibility/financial-management critiques.
persons.relationships: Symbol of the gap between AI hype and unit economics.

persons.name: Lawrence Summers
persons.role: Treasury Secretary (1999–2001); NEC Director (2009–2010); Harvard President (2001–2006)
persons.bluf: A central architect of late-1990s financial deregulation who later became the loudest establishment Cassandra on 2021 inflation. Assess with HIGH confidence his fingerprints on Gramm-Leach-Bliley (Glass-Steagall repeal) and the Commodity Futures Modernization Act (which exempted OTC derivatives), and MODERATE-HIGH that his 2021 'too much fiscal stimulus' warning was directionally correct against the Fed/Treasury consensus.
persons.drivers: Intellectual dominance; being proven right; proximity to power across administrations.
persons.worldview: Neoliberal-Keynesian hybrid: pro-market deregulation in the 1990s, but a believer in large counter-cyclical intervention and, latterly, in supply-constrained inflation risk and 'secular stagnation.'
persons.decision_style: Combative, supremely self-assured, willing to break with consensus publicly; prone to abrasive certainty.
persons.track_record: Backed OTC-derivatives exemption (CFMA 2000) and Glass-Steagall repeal (1999); opposed Brooksley Born's CFTC swaps-oversight push; warned in 2021 that ARP-scale stimulus would overheat the economy (vindicated); long associated with the 'activist Treasury issuance' critique he now partly voices.
persons.vulnerabilities: The Born clash and CFMA are cited in post-2008 and crypto-derivatives post-mortems; the Harvard presidency ended amid controversy; perceived as emblematic of the bipartisan dereg consensus he now critiques.
persons.relationships: Tied to Rubin (mentor) and Geithner (protégé); public sparring partner of the Yellen/Powell 'transitory' camp; informal advisor across Democratic administrations.
persons.outlook: His dereg legacy and his inflation warnings are both load-bearing in the macro narrative; a credibility weather-vane for elite consensus.
persons.confidence: HIGH (legislative record); MODERATE-HIGH (intent and influence interpreted).

persons.name: Wendy Gramm
persons.bluf: The CFTC chair whose final-days 1993 exemption for energy derivatives — issued days before she joined Enron's board — became the textbook case of the deregulation-to-revolving-door pipeline. Assess with HIGH confidence on the documented sequence; intent is interpreted.
persons.drivers: Free-market conviction; deregulatory mission; post-government board compensation.
persons.worldview: Austrian/Chicago-school deregulator; viewed most derivatives oversight as economically harmful friction.
persons.decision_style: Ideologically driven, willing to use rulemaking and exemptive authority aggressively to shrink CFTC reach.
persons.track_record: Granted the 1993 'Enron exemption' for OTC energy swaps; resigned the CFTC and joined Enron's board within weeks, serving on its audit committee until the 2001 collapse; later at the Mercatus Center.
persons.vulnerabilities: The exemption + immediate Enron board seat is the canonical revolving-door/regulatory-capture exhibit; the audit-committee role during the fraud is a permanent reputational anchor.
persons.relationships: Spouse of Senator Phil Gramm (co-author of the CFMA that codified the OTC-derivatives carve-out); part of the broader Gramm deregulatory legacy.
persons.outlook: A historical template for how exemptive authority + revolving door recur in today's crypto-derivatives and stablecoin debates.

persons.name: Lael Brainard
persons.role: Fed Governor (2014–2022); Fed Vice Chair (2022–2023); NEC Director (2023–2025)
persons.bluf: A dovish, regulation-forward Fed governor who became the leading internal advocate for a US central-bank digital currency and for tighter bank-climate and capital rules, then ran Biden's NEC. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence she was the Fed's principal CBDC and pro-regulation voice during the 2017–2022 dissents.
persons.drivers: Financial-stability priority; consumer protection; institutional reform.
persons.worldview: Interventionist Democrat; skeptical of light-touch supervision; favors public-option payment infrastructure (CBDC) over private stablecoins.
persons.decision_style: Detail-driven, willing to dissent solo against the Quarles deregulatory agenda; coalition-builder on the Board.
persons.track_record: Cast repeated dissents against post-2018 supervisory loosening; championed the Fed CBDC research; as NEC director helped shape Biden-era industrial/climate-finance policy.
persons.vulnerabilities: Her CBDC advocacy is a lightning rod in the stablecoin-vs-public-money debate; criticized by the right as politicizing supervision.
persons.relationships: Counterweight to Quarles/Bowman deregulators; aligned with Yellen on financial-stability framing.
persons.outlook: Her CBDC and supervision positions frame the live stablecoin/payments-rail contest.

persons.name: Randal Quarles
persons.role: Fed Vice Chair for Supervision (2017–2021); Fed Governor (2017–2021)
persons.bluf: The first-ever Fed Vice Chair for Supervision, whose 'tailoring' of post-crisis rules — raising the $50B SIFI threshold and easing liquidity/stress-test requirements for mid-size banks — is widely linked to the supervisory gaps exposed by the 2023 SVB/Signature failures. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence on the regulatory-loosening record; causal link to 2023 is contested.
persons.drivers: Deregulatory efficiency; reducing 'gold-plating' of bank rules; private-equity-aligned worldview.
persons.worldview: Market-friendly Republican; believed post-Dodd-Frank rules were excessive for non-G-SIB banks.
persons.decision_style: Methodical, lawyerly, incremental; implemented EGRRCPA's 2018 tailoring mandate enthusiastically.
persons.track_record: Led the 2018–2019 tailoring rules that relaxed standards for $100–250B banks (the SVB cohort); chaired the FSB; returned to private investing (Cynosure) afterward.
persons.vulnerabilities: His name is central to the post-SVB 'who weakened the rules' debate; Carlyle/private-equity ties feed the capture narrative.
persons.relationships: Implemented the Trump-era dereg agenda over Brainard's dissents; predecessor framing to Bowman's later supervision role.
persons.outlook: A direct line from his tailoring rules to the HTM/duration-risk and regional-bank-fragility threads.

persons.name: Daniel Tarullo
persons.role: Fed Governor (2009–2017); de facto head of bank supervision
persons.bluf: The unofficial architect of the post-2008 US bank-regulation regime — stress tests (CCAR), the G-SIB surcharge, and tougher capital — who ran Fed supervision before the role was formalized. Assess with HIGH confidence he built the framework that Quarles later loosened.
persons.drivers: Financial-stability primacy; macroprudential rigor; institutional memory of 2008.
persons.worldview: Hawkish on bank capital and systemic risk; skeptical of bank self-assessment of risk.
persons.decision_style: Intellectually forceful, technically deep, willing to impose binding constraints over industry objection.
persons.track_record: Designed and ran CCAR/DFAST stress testing; pushed the G-SIB capital surcharge and the supplementary leverage ratio; warned on exit about premature deregulation.
persons.vulnerabilities: Criticized by banks as heavy-handed; his framework's later dilution is the counter-case in the dereg debate.
persons.relationships: Predecessor/foil to Quarles and Bowman; aligned with Brainard on tough supervision.
persons.outlook: His framework is the baseline against which every subsequent loosening (and every bank failure) is measured.

persons.name: Richard Clarida
persons.bluf: Powell's vice chair and the intellectual author of the 2020 'flexible average inflation targeting' (FAIT) framework — the doctrine later blamed for the Fed's delayed 2021–2022 inflation response — whose tenure ended early amid scrutiny of personal trades during the 2020 market turmoil. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence FAIT contributed to the late liftoff.
persons.drivers: Academic legacy (the FAIT framework); reputation; market credibility.
persons.worldview: New-Keynesian; believed in pre-committing to inflation overshoots to anchor expectations near zero rates.
persons.decision_style: Cerebral, framework-driven, consensus-aligned with Powell.
persons.track_record: Co-authored the 2020 strategy review and FAIT; resigned weeks early in 2022 after disclosure of well-timed 2020 fund transactions (part of the broader Fed trading scandal).
persons.vulnerabilities: FAIT is central to the 'Fed was late' critique; the trading-disclosure episode dented the Fed's ethics standing alongside the Kaplan/Rosengren resignations.
persons.relationships: Powell ally; part of the cohort whose 2020–2021 trades forced new Fed ethics rules.
persons.outlook: FAIT's design choices are a root cause in the inflation-timing narrative.

persons.name: Stanley Fischer
persons.role: Fed Vice Chair (2014–2017); Bank of Israel Governor (2005–2013); IMF First Deputy MD (1994–2001)
persons.bluf: A towering academic central banker — MIT mentor to Bernanke and Draghi — who bridged the IMF, Bank of Israel, Citigroup, and the Fed, embodying the global monetary-policy establishment. Assess with HIGH confidence on his outsized influence over a generation of central bankers.
persons.drivers: Intellectual stewardship of the global monetary order; crisis management.
persons.worldview: Pragmatic Keynesian-monetarist synthesis; lender-of-last-resort interventionism.
persons.decision_style: Calm, authoritative, internationally networked; comfortable with unconventional policy.
persons.track_record: Steered IMF crisis programs (1990s EM crises), ran the Bank of Israel through 2008 with early easing, served as Citigroup vice chairman, then Fed vice chair under Yellen.
persons.vulnerabilities: The Citigroup interlude feeds the revolving-door critique; IMF-era austerity programs remain contested.
persons.relationships: Mentor to Bernanke, Draghi, and much of the modern central-banking elite; Yellen-era partner.
persons.outlook: A node connecting the IMF, private banking, and the Fed in the macro-establishment map.

persons.name: Kevin Warsh
persons.bluf: The youngest-ever Fed governor, a crisis-era market liaison turned persistent QE critic and perennial Fed-chair contender. Assess with MODERATE confidence he represents the hawkish, market-structure-focused wing repeatedly considered to lead the Fed.
persons.drivers: Influence over monetary direction; market credibility; positioning for the chairmanship.
persons.worldview: Hawkish; skeptical of prolonged balance-sheet expansion and of the Fed's market interventions; pro-normalization.
persons.decision_style: Market-savvy, politically attuned, media-fluent; an internal/external bridge during 2008.
persons.track_record: Served as the Fed's Wall Street liaison through the 2008 crisis; dissented toward tighter policy; left in 2011 and became a vocal QE critic; recurrently floated as a chair pick.
persons.vulnerabilities: Critics note Morgan Stanley/PE ties and inconsistency between crisis-era activism and later hawkishness.
persons.relationships: Bernanke-era colleague; connected to Republican economic circles and Stanford's Hoover network.
persons.outlook: A live candidate-in-waiting whose elevation would shift the Fed hawkish — a scenario variable.

persons.name: John C. Williams
persons.role: President, Federal Reserve Bank of New York (2018–present); permanent FOMC Vice Chair
persons.bluf: The NY Fed president — permanent FOMC vice chair and manager of the System Open Market Account — and co-author of the influential 'r-star' (neutral-rate) estimates that anchor the Fed's policy framing. Assess with HIGH confidence on his structural centrality to FOMC execution.
persons.drivers: Operational stewardship of monetary policy; analytical rigor on the neutral rate.
persons.worldview: Mainstream New-Keynesian; data-dependent; emphasizes the low long-run neutral rate.
persons.decision_style: Technocratic, low-drama, execution-focused; the FOMC's operational center of gravity.
persons.track_record: Ran the SF Fed research shop (r-star work with Holston-Laubach-Williams), then NY Fed; manages the desk that implements every FOMC decision and the repo facilities.
persons.vulnerabilities: His r-star estimates shape how 'restrictive' policy is judged — a contested analytical input; less a personal vulnerability than a framework dependency.
persons.relationships: Powell's operational right hand; permanent voter regardless of rotation.
persons.outlook: Controls policy implementation and the repo plumbing — a structural rather than rotating influence node.

persons.name: Christopher Waller
persons.bluf: A Fed governor who became the Board's most articulate stablecoin proponent and CBDC skeptic — arguing private dollar-stablecoins extend dollar dominance and that a US CBDC is a solution in search of a problem. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence he anchors the pro-private-stablecoin, anti-CBDC pole inside the Fed.
persons.drivers: Monetary-economics rigor; preserving dollar primacy via private rails; opposing payment-system state expansion.
persons.worldview: Market-oriented; sees well-regulated stablecoins as complementary to, not threatening, the dollar system.
persons.decision_style: Academic, plain-spoken, willing to stake out clear positions on crypto and rates.
persons.track_record: Published prominent speeches favoring stablecoins and questioning a CBDC; a relatively hawkish-to-neutral rates voice; frequently cited in the GENIUS Act / stablecoin-regulation debate.
persons.vulnerabilities: His pro-stablecoin stance aligns him (in critics' view) with industry interests amid the debanking and reserve-quality debates.
persons.relationships: Counterpole to Brainard's CBDC advocacy; aligned with the Trump-era pro-stablecoin policy tilt.
persons.outlook: A key Fed voice shaping whether stablecoins or a CBDC win the dollar-rail contest.

persons.name: Michelle Bowman
persons.role: Fed Vice Chair for Supervision (2025–present); Fed Governor (2018–present)
persons.bluf: The community-banker-turned-Fed-governor elevated in 2025 to Vice Chair for Supervision, signaling a deregulatory, tailoring-friendly turn in bank oversight after the 2023 failures. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence she will ease capital/supervision burdens on regional and community banks.
persons.drivers: Community-bank advocacy; reducing regulatory burden; supervisory predictability.
persons.worldview: Light-touch Republican; skeptical of one-size-fits-all capital rules and of aggressive Basel 'endgame' increases.
persons.decision_style: Pragmatic, industry-grounded, incremental; dissents toward less stringent rules.
persons.track_record: Dissented against parts of the Basel III endgame and climate-supervision moves; elevated to the supervision role in 2025 to lead a deregulatory agenda.
persons.vulnerabilities: Her elevation reopens the post-SVB 'are we loosening too soon' debate; community-bank ties feed capture concerns.
persons.relationships: Successor to the Quarles supervisory posture; counter to Brainard/Tarullo.
persons.outlook: Her agenda will set bank-capital and crypto-bank supervision for the current cycle.

persons.name: Neel Kashkari
persons.role: President, Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis (2016–present); TARP Administrator (2008–2009)
persons.bluf: The Treasury official who ran TARP at 35, later a vocal Minneapolis Fed president oscillating between dove and hawk and a persistent 'too-big-to-fail' critic. Assess with MODERATE confidence he is the FOMC's most public communicator and a TBTF-reform advocate.
persons.drivers: Public engagement; ending too-big-to-fail; being heard.
persons.worldview: Pragmatic; flips between dovish (pre-2021) and hawkish (post-2021 inflation) as data shifts; structurally skeptical of mega-bank safety.
persons.decision_style: Outspoken, media-forward, willing to publish detailed plans (the 'Minneapolis Plan' for TBTF).
persons.track_record: Administered the $700B TARP bank bailouts; ran (unsuccessfully) for California governor; as Minneapolis Fed pres pushed the TBTF-ending capital plan and shifted hawkish in 2022.
persons.vulnerabilities: The Goldman/Treasury/PIMCO arc and TARP role tie him to the bailout-establishment narrative he now critiques.
persons.relationships: Paulson-era Treasury alumnus; rotating FOMC voter.
persons.outlook: A bellwether communicator whose shifts telegraph FOMC sentiment.

persons.name: Donald Kohn
persons.bluf: A 40-year Fed careerist who was Greenspan's closest policy confidant and Bernanke's vice chair through the 2008 crisis — institutional memory personified. Assess with HIGH confidence on his role as a continuity anchor across the Greenspan-Bernanke transition and crisis response.
persons.drivers: Institutional stewardship; monetary-policy continuity; financial-stability learning.
persons.worldview: Mainstream, consensus-oriented; evolved from Greenspan-era light-touch toward post-crisis macroprudential thinking.
persons.decision_style: Quiet, deeply experienced, consensus-building; the staff-to-Board bridge.
persons.track_record: Greenspan's longtime monetary-policy chief, then crisis-era vice chair; later served on the Bank of England's Financial Policy Committee.
persons.vulnerabilities: Associated with the Greenspan-era 'maestro' regime whose light touch preceded 2008.
persons.relationships: Greenspan and Bernanke's right hand; trans-Atlantic regulatory link via the BoE FPC.
persons.outlook: A continuity node linking the pre- and post-2008 Fed regimes.

persons.name: Roger Ferguson
persons.role: Fed Vice Chair (1999–2006); CEO of TIAA (2008–2021)
persons.bluf: The Fed vice chair who was the only governor in DC on 9/11 and directed the central bank's response to keep the payment system running, later running the $1T-plus TIAA. Assess with HIGH confidence on the 9/11 crisis-management role; his post-Fed corporate-board career links monetary and corporate elites.
persons.drivers: System resilience; institutional credibility; bridging public and private finance.
persons.worldview: Mainstream, stability-focused; pragmatic crisis manager.
persons.decision_style: Steady, understated, operationally decisive under stress.
persons.track_record: Led the Fed's 9/11 liquidity and payments response; chaired the BIS Committee on the Global Financial System; ran TIAA for 13 years; serves on major corporate boards (incl. Alphabet).
persons.vulnerabilities: Corporate-board portfolio invites revolving-door framing, though without specific controversy.
persons.relationships: Greenspan-era vice chair; connector to large-cap corporate governance (Alphabet) relevant to the AI-capital map.
persons.outlook: A bridge node between the Fed, large asset management (TIAA), and Big Tech governance.

persons.name: Hester Peirce
persons.role: Commissioner, SEC (2018–present); head of the SEC Crypto Task Force (2025–present)
persons.bluf: The SEC's leading crypto advocate — 'Crypto Mom' — who proposed a token 'Safe Harbor,' dissented repeatedly against the Gensler-era enforcement-first approach, and now leads the Crypto Task Force writing the rules. Assess with HIGH confidence she is the principal internal architect of the SEC's 2025 pro-crypto pivot.
persons.drivers: Regulatory clarity over enforcement; limited-government principles; rule-of-law process.
persons.worldview: Free-market, due-process-focused; views 'regulation by enforcement' as illegitimate and innovation-killing.
persons.decision_style: Principled dissenter, prolific writer of detailed dissents; now in a rule-writing leadership role.
persons.track_record: Proposed the Token Safe Harbor (2020/2021); dissented against most Gensler crypto enforcement actions; appointed to lead the 2025 Crypto Task Force shaping registration/custody/token guidance.
persons.vulnerabilities: Critics argue her stance is industry-aligned and risks under-protecting investors amid the very failures the project documents.
persons.relationships: Ideological ally of the Atkins-led SEC and of Waller's pro-stablecoin Fed posture; foil to Crenshaw and to Gensler-era enforcement.
persons.outlook: Her task force's output will define the US crypto regulatory perimeter — central to the SEC/SDNY and stablecoin threads.

persons.name: Mark Uyeda
persons.bluf: The career SEC staffer-turned-commissioner who served as acting chair in early 2025 and rapidly began unwinding Gensler-era crypto and ESG rules before Atkins's confirmation. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence he executed the transitional deregulatory pivot at the agency.
persons.drivers: Deregulatory restoration; cost-benefit discipline; institutional process.
persons.worldview: Market-oriented Republican; skeptical of expansive disclosure mandates (climate) and enforcement-led crypto policy.
persons.decision_style: Process-driven, institutionally fluent (decades of SEC staff experience), incremental.
persons.track_record: As acting chair, paused/withdrew climate-disclosure defense, launched the crypto-rules reset, and set up the Crypto Task Force with Peirce before handing off to Atkins.
persons.vulnerabilities: Speed of the rollback draws process and investor-protection criticism.
persons.relationships: Aligned with Peirce and Atkins; transitional foil to the Gensler agenda.
persons.outlook: Helped set the trajectory the Atkins SEC now executes.

persons.name: Caroline Crenshaw
persons.bluf: The SEC's lone remaining Democratic commissioner and its most outspoken internal crypto skeptic and investor-protection hawk, dissenting against the 2025 deregulatory and crypto-accommodation turn. Assess with MODERATE-HIGH confidence she is the principal dissenting voice on the current Commission.
persons.drivers: Investor protection; market integrity; guarding against systemic crypto risk.
persons.worldview: Pro-regulation Democrat; views many crypto products as non-compliant securities posing investor harm.
persons.decision_style: Detailed, data-driven dissents; willing to be the sole 'no' vote.
persons.track_record: Dissented against Bitcoin/Ether ETF approvals and crypto-rule rollbacks; flagged stablecoin and conflict-of-interest risks; her renomination became politically contested.
persons.vulnerabilities: Politically isolated on a Republican-majority Commission; her warnings are the on-record counter-narrative.
persons.relationships: Foil to Peirce/Uyeda/Atkins; aligned in spirit with Gensler-era and Warren-style investor-protection positions.
persons.outlook: Her dissents are the documented internal-record counterweight to the crypto pivot the project tracks.

persons.name: Richard Breeden
persons.bluf: The SEC chair who handled the S&L-era enforcement wave and the Salomon Brothers Treasury-auction-rigging scandal (1991), establishing the modern template for forcing reform on a systemically important firm. Assess with HIGH confidence on his role setting precedents for enforcement against market-rigging.
persons.drivers: Market integrity; aggressive enforcement; reform of compromised institutions.
persons.worldview: Enforcement-forward Republican; believed in tough sanctions to deter manipulation.
persons.decision_style: Assertive, willing to impose leadership changes (e.g., the Buffett-led Salomon cleanup).
persons.track_record: Drove the Salomon Treasury-auction-manipulation resolution; strengthened enforcement post-S&L; later a corporate-monitor and activist investor (WorldCom monitor).
persons.vulnerabilities: Era predates current tools; mainly of historical-precedent relevance.
persons.relationships: Bush-41 appointee; the Salomon case is a template echoed in later market-plumbing manipulation cases.
persons.outlook: Historical precedent node for enforcement against auction/market rigging relevant to the plumbing thread.

persons.name: David Ruder
persons.bluf: The SEC chair during the October 1987 'Black Monday' crash, whose handling of the program-trading and circuit-breaker aftermath shaped modern market-structure safeguards. Assess with HIGH confidence on his role in the post-1987 market-structure reforms.
persons.drivers: Market stability; understanding the 1987 crash mechanics; reform.
persons.worldview: Academic securities-law scholar; measured, study-driven.
persons.decision_style: Deliberative, professorial; emphasized inter-agency coordination (the SEC-CFTC jurisdictional fault line surfaced in 1987).
persons.track_record: Led the SEC through Black Monday; the crash's program-trading findings fed circuit breakers and the SEC/CFTC coordination debate.
persons.vulnerabilities: Limited modern footprint; historical-precedent relevance.
persons.relationships: His era exposed the SEC-CFTC split that recurs in today's crypto-derivatives jurisdiction fights.
persons.outlook: Historical node for market-structure fragility and the SEC/CFTC turf question.

persons.name: Harold Williams
persons.bluf: The Carter-era SEC chair who emphasized corporate-governance reform — board independence and audit-committee standards — laying groundwork still referenced in disclosure and governance debates. Assess with MODERATE confidence on his lasting governance-reform influence.
persons.drivers: Corporate governance; board accountability; disclosure quality.
persons.worldview: Reform-minded; believed independent boards and audit committees were the front line of investor protection.
persons.decision_style: Principled, governance-focused, academic-leaning.
persons.track_record: Pushed audit-committee and board-independence norms; later led the Getty Trust.
persons.vulnerabilities: Pre-modern era; mainly governance-precedent relevance.
persons.relationships: His governance agenda foreshadows today's audit-committee and disclosure-integrity concerns (cf. the Wendy Gramm/Enron audit-committee failure).
persons.outlook: Historical governance-reform node.

persons.name: James Newsome
persons.bluf: The CFTC chair who oversaw early-2000s energy-derivatives markets in the post-CFMA, post-Enron period, then immediately became president of NYMEX — a clean revolving-door exemplar in the commodity-exchange space. Assess with HIGH confidence on the chronology.
persons.drivers: Market development; light-touch commodity oversight; industry alignment.
persons.worldview: Deregulatory commodities regulator; favored exchange self-regulation.
persons.decision_style: Industry-friendly, pragmatic, transition-minded.
persons.track_record: Chaired the CFTC through the Enron aftermath without major re-regulation, then ran NYMEX (the dominant energy exchange) within months of leaving.
persons.vulnerabilities: The CFTC-to-NYMEX move is a textbook revolving-door case alongside Wendy Gramm's.
persons.relationships: Successor to the Gramm-era CFTC posture; part of the commodity-exchange capture pattern.
persons.outlook: Historical revolving-door node relevant to commodity-market plumbing and oversight.

persons.name: Walter Lukken
persons.bluf: The acting CFTC chair through the 2008 commodity-price spike and financial crisis, who then became the long-running head of the global futures-industry lobby (FIA) — embodying the regulator-to-industry-association pipeline. Assess with HIGH confidence on the role sequence.
persons.drivers: Derivatives-market functioning; industry representation; clearing-system advocacy.
persons.worldview: Market-oriented; champions central clearing and the futures-industry model.
persons.decision_style: Technocratic, industry-fluent, relationship-driven.
persons.track_record: Led the CFTC during the 2008 oil spike and Lehman; later ran the FIA, lobbying on clearing, capital, and crypto-derivatives rules.
persons.vulnerabilities: The CFTC-to-FIA arc is a capture-narrative exhibit; FIA's positions track member-firm interests.
persons.relationships: Bridge between CFTC alumni and the clearing/derivatives industry relevant to the market-plumbing thread.
persons.outlook: Industry-lobby node connecting regulation and the clearing complex.

persons.name: Reuben Jeffery III
persons.role: Chair, CFTC (2005–2007); Under Secretary of State for Economic Affairs (2007–2009)
persons.bluf: The Goldman-Sachs-partner-turned-CFTC-chair-turned-State-Department-economic-chief who personifies the Goldman-to-government circuit running through commodity-market regulation. Assess with HIGH confidence on the career arc; influence interpreted.
persons.drivers: Public service from a Wall Street base; market-friendly regulation; economic diplomacy.
persons.worldview: Establishment Republican; market-oriented; internationally networked.
persons.decision_style: Polished, relationship-driven, transition-fluent across finance, regulation, and diplomacy.
persons.track_record: Goldman partner before chairing the CFTC, then Under Secretary of State for Economic Affairs, later CEO of Rockefeller & Co.
persons.vulnerabilities: The Goldman/CFTC/State/Rockefeller path is a dense revolving-door/network exhibit.
persons.relationships: Part of the Goldman-government network alongside Paulson, Bannon-era figures, and others in the roster.
persons.outlook: Network node linking Goldman, commodity regulation, and economic statecraft.

persons.name: Changpeng Zhao
persons.bluf: Founder of the world's largest crypto exchange, who pleaded guilty in 2023 to Bank Secrecy Act violations (failure to maintain an effective anti-money-laundering program) in a $4.3B DOJ/Treasury settlement and served four months. Assess with HIGH confidence on the guilty plea and settlement; his post-release re-emergence is a live variable.
persons.drivers: Market dominance; the 'CZ' personal brand; protecting Binance's global liquidity franchise.
persons.worldview: Crypto-maximalist, jurisdiction-arbitraging; long operated Binance without a fixed legal home ('Binance has no headquarters').
persons.decision_style: Fast, centralizing, founder-dominant; reportedly ran AML/compliance as secondary to growth.
persons.track_record: Built Binance into the dominant exchange; the 2023 plea and settlement is the largest crypto enforcement action to date; his November 2022 tweets helped trigger the FTX bank-run; remains BNB's largest holder and an active investor.
persons.vulnerabilities: The BSA conviction; persistent regulatory exposure across jurisdictions; concentration of BNB holdings.
